gallery-plate.$description: Gallery Plate — Primary canvas and hairline dividers — the infinite gray field that hosts floating objects and borders nearly every surface in the system

carbon.$description: Carbon — Primary text, icon strokes, and the dark pill in the nav bar — the only true black in the system, used for type and high-contrast marks

chalk.$description: Chalk — Elevated surface above the canvas — cards, panels, and lighter button fills that need to lift off the gray field

paper-white.$description: Paper White — Inset surfaces and inverse text on dark fills — the cleanest white available for maximum contrast pop

concrete.$description: Concrete — Tertiary surface and muted button fill — one step darker than Chalk, used where a surface needs weight without drama

voltage-lime.$description: Voltage Lime — Primary action fill and highlight accent — the only chromatic button/link background in the system, applied to the Menu pill and active link states to switch a control on

oxide-brown.$description: Oxide Brown — Decorative content accent — appears inside imagery and printed artifacts (labels, type on objects); not a UI color but a recurring editorial note

abcmonumentgrotesk.$description: Sole typeface across all UI — body, labels, nav, headings, buttons, icons, links. The single medium weight with uniformly negative tracking (~-0.48px) gives every label a compressed, display-poster feel regardless of size. Avoid pairing with a secondary face; the constraint IS the identity.

gallery-plate.$description: Surface level 0: Page canvas — the infinite gray field

paper-white.$description: Surface level 1: Inset highlight or inverse text background

chalk.$description: Surface level 2: Elevated card, nav bar fill, neutral button

concrete.$description: Surface level 3: Pressed/hovered neutral state, secondary surface

voltage-lime.$description: Surface level 4: Highlighted surface and primary action fill
